N.description: Number of existing hubs that must remain open (hubs 0..N-1)

M.description: Total number of hub locations (existing and potential new)

v.description: Commuter demand from junction s to POI p

F.description: Feasibility indicator: 1 iff junction-hub-POI triple is viable for Park and Bike use

U.description: Maximum number of new hubs that can be opened

code.python: assert all(v[s][p] >= 0 for s in range(nS) for p in range(nP))

assumptions.formulation: F_{shp} \in \{0, 1\} \quad \forall s \in S, h \in H, p \in P

code.python: assert all(F[s][h][p] in (0, 1) for s in range(nS) for h in range(M) for p in range(nP))

assumptions.description: The number of existing hubs does not exceed total hubs.
The maximum number of new hubs is non-negative.
Number of Points of Interest is positive.
Number of junction roads is positive.
Total number of hub locations is positive.

y.description: 1 if hub h is opened, 0 otherwise

z.description: 1 if demand from junction s to POI p is covered by at least one open feasible hub, 0 otherwise

constraints.description: At most U new hubs (indices N..M-1) may be opened.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(y[h] for h in range(N, M)) <= U)

constraints.description: All existing hubs (indices 0..N-1) must remain open.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(y[h] for h in range(N)) == N)

constraints.description: Demand from junction s to POI p can only be covered if at least one feasible open hub exists for the pair.
constraints.formulation: z_{sp} \leq \sum_{h \in H} F_{shp} \, y_h \quad \forall s \in S, p \in P

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(z[s, p] <= gp.quicksum(F[s][h][p] * y[h] for h in range(M)) for s in range(nS) for p in range(nP))

objective.description: Maximize the total commuter demand covered by the hub network.

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(v[s][p] * z[s, p] for s in range(nS) for p in range(nP)),
    GRB.MAXIMIZE
)
